code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test05-JUL-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity. Urine is brown and cloudy with ammoniacal odor, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular dysfunction. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and etiology.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
